bullets.text: Full-year 2009 combined ratio was 91.6%, well inside the 96 target; ROE was 21.4% and net income exceeded $1 billion, a dramatic reversal from the $70 million net loss of 2008.

Investment portfolio achieved a 12.5% fully taxable equivalent total return for 2009; comprehensive income was $1.75 billion versus a comprehensive loss of $615 million in 2008; unrealized gains swung to nearly $700 million.

Book value per share increased 37% to $8.55; debt-to-total capital returned to guideline at 27.5%; Progressive repurchased 11.1 million shares and declared a record variable dividend of $0.1613/share plus proposed a 25% underwriting income target for 2010.

Net premiums written surpassed $14 billion for 2009; Direct auto grew 13% in policies and 11% in written premium; Agency auto saw net policy count reverse a multi-year declining trend and turn slightly positive.

Progressive served over one million more Personal Lines policies than in 2006, with 639,000 additional auto customers; multi-product household policies grew over 100% in 2009, approaching a million combined-product customers.

Direct channel is now ~43% of personal auto policies versus Agency at ~57%; both channels produced combined ratios between 93-94 for personal auto; online quoting leadership confirmed alongside GEICO as well ahead of all others.

Year-end run-rate Loss Adjustment Expense ratio declined approximately one full point from prior year; claims restructuring benefits from nearly a decade of process improvement were fully realized in 2009.
'Flo' and the Superstore campaign generated strong consumer recognition and appeal; brand ambassadorship from employees ensures congruence between marketing messaging and customer experience.

Name Your Price completed countrywide rollout; progressive.com earned Forrester's best Brand Building Web site across all financial services; new policy processing system went live in first state with excellent quality.
Progressive launched an Internet-only personal auto business in Australia; sold Professional Liability Group to American Bankers Association affiliate to focus on core strategic interests.
